citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 605.F
summary: Complying with this county code doesn't excuse compliance with other state or municipal law. Where this code conflicts with another applicable law, the more stringent (protective) provision controls.
full_text: Compliance with the provisions of this Article does not eliminate the responsibility of complying with any other applicable state, county or municipal law, ordinance or regulation. Where a provision of this Article conflicts with a provision of any other applicable state, county or municipal law, ordinance or regulation, the more stringent provision shall control.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 606.B
summary: A tenant must give the landlord access at reasonable times to make repairs needed to comply with the county housing code.
full_text: The occupant of any dwelling, dwelling unit or rooming unit shall give the owner, operator or their agent access to any part of such dwelling, dwelling unit or rooming unit, or its premises, at all reasonable times, for the purpose of making repairs or alterations necessary to comply with the provisions of this Article.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 621-625
summary: The landlord must maintain the structural soundness of the building, keep stairs and handrails safe, equip exterior entrance doors with deadbolt locks, install fall-prevention devices on certain upper-floor windows, and ensure every unit has safe, unobstructed egress.
full_text: Every foundation, floor, wall, ceiling and roof shall be structurally sound and maintained in good repair. Every stairway, ramp, porch and appurtenance thereto shall be maintained in a structurally sound condition and in good repair, and shall be provided with handrails where required. Every entrance door leading directly to the outdoors shall be equipped with a deadbolt lock. Windows in dwelling units located above the first story shall be equipped with an approved fall prevention device where required by this Article. Every dwelling unit shall have safe, continuous and unobstructed means of egress.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 626-627
summary: The property must be graded and drained to prevent standing water or landslide hazards, and any accessory structures (sheds, garages, etc.) must be kept in safe, sanitary condition.
full_text: Premises shall be graded and properly drained so as to prevent the accumulation of stagnant water and to prevent water damage or a landslide hazard. Accessory structures on the premises shall be maintained in a safe and sanitary condition.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 629-630
summary: Every unit must have properly installed, safely maintained electrical service. Outlets near sinks and exterior outlets must have ground fault circuit interrupter (GFCI) protection, and hallways/stairways must have adequate lighting.
full_text: Every dwelling unit shall be supplied with electrical service, outlets and fixtures properly installed and maintained in good and safe working condition. Any receptacle outlet installed within six feet of the outside edge of a sink shall be protected by a ground fault circuit interrupter. Exterior convenience electrical outlets readily accessible to occupants shall likewise be so protected. Every hallway, communicating corridor and stairway shall have adequate lighting provided at all times, or shall have conveniently located light switches.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 631.A-C
summary: The county sets minimum indoor temperature standards a rental unit must be able to maintain, generally around 68 degrees Fahrenheit in habitable rooms, with a lower threshold of around 61 degrees during colder outdoor conditions. Note: confirm the exact outdoor-temperature trigger points against the source, this entry's numbers are drawn from the code but the precise trigger thresholds are worth double-checking.
full_text: Every owner or operator shall supply sufficient heat to every dwelling unit to maintain a temperature of at least 68 degrees Fahrenheit in all habitable rooms, bathrooms and toilet rooms during the heating season, and shall maintain a temperature of at least 61 degrees Fahrenheit during periods of extreme cold, in accordance with the temperature and seasonal thresholds specified in this section.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 632-634
summary: Every unit must be connected to an approved water supply, and hot water must be available at a minimum of 110 degrees Fahrenheit.
full_text: Every dwelling unit shall be connected to an approved public or private water supply. Every dwelling unit shall have supplied water-heating facilities capable of heating water to a temperature of at least 110 degrees Fahrenheit, delivered to fixtures requiring hot water.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 635-637
summary: Every unit must have a kitchen sink and a private bathroom with a flush toilet and a bathtub or shower, both connected to hot and cold running water.
full_text: Every dwelling unit shall contain a kitchen sink in good working condition, and a room affording privacy to a person within, containing a flush toilet and a bathtub or shower, all connected to an approved hot and cold running water supply.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 638
summary: By default, a tenant must keep their unit clean and sanitary, properly dispose of trash, keep plumbing fixtures clean, and maintain required clearance around furnaces, boilers, and water heaters, even if the lease doesn't say so.
full_text: Every occupant shall keep the dwelling unit and any premises exclusively under their control in a clean and sanitary condition, shall properly dispose of rubbish and garbage, shall keep plumbing fixtures in a clean condition, and shall maintain required clearance around furnaces, boilers and water heaters.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 640-642
summary: Bathrooms must afford privacy, have a window or mechanical ventilation to the outdoors, and have a floor surface that can be kept clean and sanitary.
full_text: Every bathroom shall afford privacy to a person within, shall be equipped with a window opening to the outdoors or a mechanical ventilation system discharging to the outdoors, and shall have a floor surface capable of being easily maintained in a clean and sanitary condition.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 643.A, D.1
summary: Smoke detectors and carbon monoxide detectors must be installed and kept in working condition on every story of the dwelling unit, including basements and cellars.
full_text: Single and multiple-station smoke detectors shall be of an approved type and shall be installed and maintained in full operating condition in each story within the dwelling unit, including basements and cellars. Single and multiple-station carbon monoxide detectors shall likewise be installed and maintained in full operating condition in each story within the dwelling unit, including basements and cellars.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 644
summary: A bedroom cannot be accessible only by walking through another bedroom or through a bathroom.
full_text: No sleeping room shall be accessible only by passing through another sleeping room or a bathroom.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 645-646
summary: A basement or cellar room used as a bedroom or living space must meet minimum window/egress size requirements, and habitable rooms generally need at least 84 inches of ceiling height (with a partial exception for sloped ceilings).
full_text: A basement or cellar space used as a habitable room shall have a window area meeting the minimum egress size requirements of this Article. The ceiling height of any habitable room, hallway, corridor, laundry area, bathroom or toilet room shall be at least 84 inches, except that in any habitable room with a sloping ceiling, at least one-third of the floor area shall have a ceiling height of at least 84 inches.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 647-648
summary: A dwelling unit needs at least 150 square feet of floor area for the first occupant, plus 100 more square feet per additional occupant. A sleeping room needs at least 70 square feet for one occupant, plus 50 more square feet per additional occupant. Especially relevant for shared student rentals with multiple roommates.
full_text: Every dwelling unit shall have at least 150 square feet of floor area for the first occupant and at least 100 additional square feet of floor area for each additional occupant. Every room occupied for sleeping purposes shall contain at least 70 square feet of floor area for one occupant and at least 50 additional square feet of floor area for each additional occupant.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 649-650
summary: Habitable rooms need a window to the outdoors with glass area of at least 8 percent of the floor area, and bathrooms need adequate ventilation to the outdoors.
full_text: Every habitable room shall have at least one window facing directly to the outdoors, with a total window area of not less than eight percent of the floor area of the room. Every bathroom or toilet room, if mechanically ventilated, must provide a minimum of six air changes per hour, discharged to the outdoors and not recirculated.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Section 651
summary: If the county identifies a lead hazard (like deteriorated lead paint or a chewable painted surface), the landlord must abate it. Separately, applying lead-based paint to any surface of a dwelling is flatly prohibited. This is a local, county-level duty, distinct from the federal lead-paint disclosure requirement for pre-1978 housing.
full_text: Where the Department identifies a lead hazard in a dwelling, including deteriorated paint or a chewable surface coated with lead-based paint, the owner shall be required to abate the hazard. No person shall apply lead-based paint to any interior or exterior surface of a dwelling.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 652-654
summary: Rental properties must be kept rodent-proof and pest-free. In multi-unit buildings, the landlord handles pest control affecting shared areas or multiple units. In a single-family rental, the tenant is generally responsible for pest control, unless the landlord's failure to maintain the property caused the infestation.
full_text: Every dwelling shall be maintained in a rodent-proof and pest-free condition. In a dwelling containing two or more dwelling units, the owner shall be responsible for extermination where infestation exists in two or more units or in shared or public areas. In a single-family dwelling, the occupant shall be responsible for extermination, except where the infestation is caused by the owner's failure to maintain the dwelling in a rodent-proof condition.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 655-656
summary: Tenants must dispose of trash in approved containers. In multi-unit buildings, the landlord is responsible for supplying approved trash containers for shared or common areas.
full_text: Every occupant shall dispose of rubbish, garbage and other organic waste in approved containers. In a dwelling containing two or more dwelling units, the owner shall be responsible for supplying approved containers for the disposal of waste from shared or common areas.

citation: Allegheny County Health Dept. Rules and Regulations, Article VI, Sections 658-659
summary: Tenants must keep the part of the property they occupy or control clean and sanitary. The landlord is responsible for keeping shared or public areas of the building clean and sanitary.
full_text: Every occupant shall keep that part of the dwelling and premises which they occupy or control in a clean and sanitary condition. The owner shall be responsible for maintaining shared or public areas of the dwelling in a clean and sanitary condition.
